zh.lifeSummary: 當Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare於1457年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達哈利斯托時，她的父親Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester42歲，母親Margaret Maude d'Artois25歲。她於大約1470年與Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare在愛爾蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和9個女兒的父母。她去世於1495年11月22日在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達梅諾斯享年38歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達吉爾倫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare于1457年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達哈利斯托时，她的父亲Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester42岁，母亲Margaret Maude d'Artois25岁。她于大约1470年与Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare在爱尔兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和9个女儿的父母。她去世于1495年11月22日在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達梅諾斯享年38岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達吉爾倫。

nameMeanings.name: FitzGerald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Jarrell
meanings: Irish: altered form of   Fitzgerald  .
English (Kent and Middlesex; of Norman origin): variant of Jarrold, itself a variant of Gerald .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Eustace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Eustace (male and female), via Old French Eustace (male) from Latin Eustachius, Eustacius, from Greek Eustachios, composed of the elements eu ‘good’ and stachys ‘ear (of grain)’, hence ‘fruitful’. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, but little is known of the most famous Saint Eustace, patron saint of hunters, said to have been converted by the vision of a crucifix between the antlers of a hunted stag. In North America, this surname form of the name may have absorbed Greek surnames based on the similar personal name Efstathios, such as Efstathiadis and Efstathiou . See also Stacey .

variants.name: Heustis
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Eustace  .

variants.name: Estacio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Manuel, Adolfo, Aida, Benigno, Carolina, Celedonio, Cosme, Edgardo, Emilio, Enrique.
Portuguese, Galician, and Spanish: variant of Eustacio (see Eustace ).

variants.name: Eustice
meanings: English (Cornwall): variant of   Eustace  .

variants.name: Eustache
meanings: French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Eustache, from Latin Eustachius (see Eustace ). This surname was brought to the US mainly from Haiti.

variants.name: Astacio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Miguel, Jorge, Julio, Justo, Luis, Aida, Alberto, Andres, Angel, Carlos, Domingo.

Spanish: probably from a variant of the personal name Estacio (Latin Eustachius; see Eustace ). This surname is found mainly in the Dominican Republic. In Spain, it is concentrated in Alcalá de Guadaira, in Seville province.

variants.name: Eustaquio
meanings: Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Eustaquio (see Eustace ).

variants.name: Eustis
meanings: English (Glamorgan): variant of   Eustace  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Efstathiou
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the genitive case of the personal name Efstathios, classical Greek Eustathios ‘well-grounded, firm, steadfast’ (from eu ‘well’ + stathēs ‘standing’, a derivative of histēmi ‘to stand’). Eustathios was the name of several saints and patriarchs of the Eastern Church. In the early Middle Ages this name became confused in the Western Church with Eustachios ‘fruitful’ (see Eustace ).

variants.name: Huestis
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of English   Eustace  . Compare   Heustis  .

nameMeanings.name: Alison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a very common medieval name, a Norman French diminutive of   Alice .  Despite its early popularity, it became quite rare in England in the 16th century. However, it survived in Scotland, with the result that until its revival in England in the 20th century it had a strongly Scottish flavour.

variants.name: Alyson
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alison .

variants.name: Allyson
meanings: Respelling of   Alison .

variants.name: Allison
meanings: Respelling of   Alison .  This is the more common form of the name in the United States.

variants.name: Alysoun
meanings: Medieval spelling of   Alison  , which has enjoyed some currency since the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare was born in 1457, in Harristown, County Kildare, Ireland, her father, Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester, was 42 and her mother, Margaret Maude d'Artois, was 25. She married Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare about 1470, in Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 9 daughters. She died on 22 November 1495, in Maynooth, County Kildare, Ireland, at the age of 38, and was buried in Kilcullen, County Kildare, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare est née en 1457, à Harristown, County Kildare, Irlande, son père, Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Margaret Maude d'Artois, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare environ 1470, à Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 9 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 novembre 1495, à Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlande, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à Kilcullen, County Kildare, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare 1457 in Harristown, County Kildare, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester 42 und ihre Mutter Margaret Maude d'Artois 25. Sie hat um 1470 in Irland Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 9 Töchter. Sie starb am 22. November 1495 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Maynooth, County Kildare, Irland und wurde in Kilcullen, County Kildare, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare è nata il nel 1457 nel Harristown, County Kildare, Irlanda, sua padre, Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Margaret Maude d'Artois, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare circa 1470, nel Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 9 figlie. Morì il 22 novembre 1495, nel Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlanda, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolta a Kilcullen, County Kildare, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildareが1457年にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアハーリストウンで生まれた時，父Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlesterは42歳，母Margaret Maude d'Artoisは25歳であった。約1470年にアイルランドでGerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼女は1495年11月22日にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアメイノオスで38歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ キルダアキルクウレンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare(이)가 1457년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 해리스타운에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Maude d'Artois(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1470년 아일랜드에서 Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1495년 11월 22일 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 메이노스에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 킬컬렌에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare nasceu em 1457, em Harristown, County Kildare, Irlanda, seu pai, Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Maude d'Artois, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare aproximadamente 1470, em Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 9 filhas. Ela faleceu em 22 de novembro de 1495, em Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlanda, com 38 anos, e foi sepultada em Kilcullen, County Kildare, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare родилась в 1457 г. в местности харристоун, коунти каилдар, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Maude d'Artois, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare около 1470 г. в местности Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 9 дочерей. Она умерла 22 ноября 1495 г. в местности меинут, коунти каилдар, Ирландия в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности килкуллен, коунти каилдар, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alison FitzGerald or Eustace Countess of Kildare nació en 1457, en Harristown, County Kildare, Irlanda, su papá, Rowland Eustace 1st and last Baron Portlester, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Margaret Maude d'Artois, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Gerald FitzMaurice FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare aproximadamente 1470, en Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 9 hijas. Murió el 22 de noviembre de 1495, en Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlanda, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en Kilcullen, County Kildare, Irlanda.
